emotion.help.step1: Look at the different emojis and choose the one that best matches how you feel right now.

emotion.help.step3: If you want to pick a different one, click the Reset button in the header or the Change button.

soundlevel.help.step3: Drag the Blue Dot on the circle to set the noise limit.
soundlevel.help.step4: If it gets too loud, the shield will turn Red and show an alert.

clock.help.title: How to Use the Clock
clock.help.step1: Click Time Now to see the real time, or Set Time to change it.
clock.help.step2: Move the clock hands or use the arrows on the numbers.
clock.help.step3: The black hand is for hours, the blue hand is for minutes, and the red hand is for seconds.
stopwatch.help.title: Stopwatch
stopwatch.help.step1: Click the Main Button to start or pause the stopwatch.
stopwatch.help.step2: Use the Flag Icon to record a lap point.
stopwatch.help.step3: The History on the right shows your lap times.
stopwatch.history.title: Lap History
countdown.help.title: How to Use the Countdown
countdown.help.step1: Use the arrows above and below the numbers to set your time.
countdown.help.step2: Click the visualization or the main digits to start and pause.
countdown.help.step3: Open Config to change the Visual Mode (Circle, Sand, Grid, or Rainbow).
countdown.help.step4: Pick an Alarm Sound to play when the time reaches zero.
examclock.help.title: How to Use the Exam Clock
examclock.help.step1: Set the Name and times for reading, the exam, and the warning.
examclock.help.step2: Click Start Reading to begin the first countdown.
examclock.help.step3: After reading, the clock waits. Click Start Exam to begin the main time.
examclock.help.step4: The clock turns Red when the warning time is reached.
examclock.setup.title: New Exam
examclock.setup.name: Exam Name
dailyschedule.help.title: How to Use the Daily Schedule
dailyschedule.help.step1: Switch between Days of the week at the top to manage different schedules.
dailyschedule.help.step2: Click Add Activity to insert a new lesson. You can set the start and end times manually.
dailyschedule.help.step3: Tap the Emoji icon to change the visual representation of the activity.
dailyschedule.help.step4: The schedule highlights the current activity based on your local system clock.
hundredschart.help.title: How to Use the Hundreds Chart
hundredschart.help.step1: Pick a color and click any number to highlight it.
hundredschart.help.step2: Use the Black color to hide numbers for a guessing game!
hundredschart.help.step3: Click the Quick Highlight buttons to see patterns of multiples (like 2, 5, or 10).
hundredschart.help.step4: Adjust the Animation Speed to see patterns appear at different speeds.
abacus.help.title: How to Use the Abacus
abacus.help.step1: Click beads to move them towards the center bar to count.
abacus.help.step4: Each column represents a different value, starting from 1s on the left up to 1,000,000,000s!
fractionwall.help.title: How to Use the Fraction Wall
fractionwall.help.step1: Click on the Fraction Buttons at the top to add or remove rows from the wall.
fractionwall.help.step2: Tap any Brick in the wall to highlight it. The system will automatically show Equivalent Fractions in other rows.
fractionwall.help.step3: Rows higher up represent larger parts, while rows lower down represent smaller parts.
fractionwall.help.step4: A Full Wall equals exactly 1 whole. Use this to visualize how different fractions relate to each other.
metronome.help.title: How to Use the Metronome
metronome.help.step1: Adjust the BPM (Beats Per Minute) using the slider or the plus/minus buttons.
metronome.help.step2: Click the large Start button to begin the steady beat.
metronome.help.step3: The Beat Indicators at the top will flash to show the current position in the 4-beat cycle.
metronome.help.step4: The first beat of every cycle has a higher pitch to help you keep track of the downbeat.
colourHunt.help.step1: Pick a Time Limit (30s, 1m, or 2m) for the hunt.
colourHunt.help.step2: Click Start Hunt to reveal a random target color.
colourHunt.help.step3: Students must find an object of that color in the classroom before the timer runs out!
colourHunt.help.step4: You can stop the timer early or click Play Again to find a new color.
guessingGame.help.title: How to Play the Guessing Game
guessingGame.help.step2: Look at the Emoji and pick the correct name.
guessingGame.help.step3: Be quick! You only have 10 seconds per question.
guessingGame.title: Guessing Game
guessingGame.subtitle: Guess the emoji!
reactionTime.help.title: How to Use Reaction Time
reactionTime.help.step1: Click the large Start Test area to begin the sequence.
reactionTime.help.step2: The screen will turn Red—this means WAIT. Don't click yet!
reactionTime.help.step3: When the screen turns Green, click as fast as you possibly can!
reactionTime.help.step4: Check your Stats in the sidebar to see your average and best reaction times.
reactionTime.status.waiting.subtext: Wait for the color to change
reactionTime.history.title: History
dashboard.section.teachertools.title: Teacher Tools
dashboard.section.classroomgames.title: Classroom Games
dashboard.section.studenttools.title: Student Tools
dashboard.desc.teacherTools: Everything you need to manage and inspire your classroom.
dashboard.desc.classroomGames: Fun and educational games to boost engagement.
dashboard.desc.studentTools: Interactive tools designed to empower student learning.
about.title: About ClassRex
about.privacy.title: 100% Privacy by Design
about.privacy.desc: ClassRex is built with a "Privacy First" philosophy. Unlike many modern web apps, we don't use cookies, trackers, or cloud databases. Everything you create—from student lists to customized tools—stays exclusively on your device's Local Storage.
about.speed.title: Fast, Reliable & Offline
about.speed.desc: Because no data is sent to a server, ClassRex is lightning fast. It works 100% offline once loaded, ensuring your classroom flow is never interrupted by poor connectivity.
about.speed.dino: ClassRex is an homage to the resilience of the Chrome Dino—providing a reliable experience even when there is no internet access. Just like the Dino keeps going, so does your classroom!
about.teachers.title: Built for Teachers
about.teachers.desc: ClassRex is a collection of high-fidelity interactive tools designed to make classroom management and teaching more engaging. From Random Name Pickers to Science Simulations, every tool is crafted for the modern classroom.
about.support.title: Support the Project
about.support.desc: ClassRex is free to use and ad-free. If you find these tools helpful and would like to support their development, please consider becoming a patron.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1.title: 1. Data Storage & Local Processing
privacy.section1.desc1: ClassRex is designed with a "Privacy First" and "Local First" architecture. All data you enter into the application (such as student names, custom word lists, class schedules, and settings) is stored locally on your device using your browser's Local Storage.
privacy.section1.desc2: We do not have access to this data, nor is it ever transmitted to our servers or any third-party databases. You have complete ownership and control over your classroom data.
privacy.section2.title: 2. Cloud Services & Analytics
privacy.section2.desc1: ClassRex does not use cloud databases for storing user-generated content. We do not use invasive tracking pixels. The application is served statically, meaning it can function entirely offline once loaded.
privacy.section2.desc2: As with most website hosting services, standard server logs are maintained by our hosting provider. These logs may include IP addresses, browser types, and access times for security and maintenance purposes, but they are not linked to your classroom data.
privacy.section3.title: 3. Cookies and Tracking
privacy.section3.desc: No cookies are used at all on ClassRex. We do not use advertising cookies, third-party trackers, or cross-site tracking scripts. Browser local storage is used strictly to save your settings and classroom data on your own device.
privacy.section4.title: 4. Regulatory Compliance (COPPA, GDPR, FERPA, APPs)
privacy.section4.desc: ClassRex is an educational tool designed for teachers to use in the classroom. Because all data is stored locally on the teacher's device and no personal information is transmitted to our servers, ClassRex inherently complies with:
privacy.section4.coppa: COPPA: We do not collect personal information from children under 13.
privacy.section4.gdpr: GDPR: We act as a "Zero-Knowledge" provider; you remain the sole controller of your data.
privacy.section4.ferpa: FERPA: Student records remain within the school's control on the teacher's device.
privacy.section4.apps: Australian Privacy Principles (APPs): We satisfy the 13 APPs under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), particularly regarding anonymity (APP 2) and data security (APP 11).
privacy.section5.title: 5. Your Rights (Access, Portability, Erasure)
privacy.section5.desc: Under global privacy regulations, you have the following rights, which ClassRex facilitates through its "Configuration" tool:
privacy.section5.access: Right to Access & Portability: You can download a complete copy of all your data at any time via the "Export Backup" feature.
privacy.section5.erasure: Right to Erasure (The "Right to be Forgotten"): You can permanently delete all data stored by ClassRex using the "Clear Database" button in the settings.
privacy.section5.rectification: Right to Rectification: You can directly edit or update any information (such as class lists or words) within the application.
privacy.section6.title: 6. Changes to this Policy
privacy.section6.desc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected on this page. Because we do not collect your email address or contact information, we cannot notify you directly of changes; please review this page periodically.
privacy.contact.title: Contact
privacy.contact.desc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how ClassRex handles data, please reach out to us via our Patreon page.
gdpr.title: We Value Your Privacy
gdpr.desc: ClassRex uses your browser's local storage to save your settings, class lists, and custom tools directly on your device. We do not use cookies for tracking or send any data to our servers. Do you accept the use of local storage?
fractionTool.help.step1: Use the arrows to change the top number (numerator) and bottom number (denominator).
